name: WBC
clinical_significance: White blood cell count. Elevated in infection, inflammation, stress, leukemia. Low in immunosuppression, bone marrow failure. PhenoAge component (coefficient 0.0954).
text_chunk: WBC (4.5-11.0 x10^3/µL) is a PhenoAge biological age component. Leukocytosis (>11.0) suggests infection or inflammation. Leukopenia (<4.5) suggests immunosuppression. PhenoAge coefficient 0.0954 (higher WBC accelerates biological age).

name: RBC
clinical_significance: Red blood cell count. Low in anemia (iron deficiency, B12/folate deficiency, chronic disease). High in polycythemia, dehydration, chronic hypoxia.
text_chunk: RBC (4.5-5.5 x10^6/µL male, 4.0-5.0 female) reflects erythropoiesis. Low values indicate anemia; high values suggest polycythemia or dehydration. HFE mutations affect iron-dependent erythropoiesis.

name: Hemoglobin
clinical_significance: Oxygen-carrying protein. Low in all anemias. WHO anemia thresholds: <13 g/dL male, <12 g/dL female. High in polycythemia vera, chronic hypoxia.
text_chunk: Hemoglobin (13.5-17.5 g/dL male, 12.0-16.0 female). WHO defines anemia as <13 male, <12 female. Iron deficiency is most common cause. HFE C282Y homozygotes may have elevated hemoglobin from iron overload.

name: Hematocrit
clinical_significance: Percentage of blood volume occupied by red blood cells. Low in anemia, overhydration. High in polycythemia, dehydration.
text_chunk: Hematocrit (38-50% male, 36-44% female) represents the fraction of blood composed of RBCs. Low values parallel hemoglobin in diagnosing anemia. Elevated values may indicate dehydration or polycythemia.

name: MCV
clinical_significance: Mean corpuscular volume. Microcytic (<80): iron deficiency, thalassemia. Macrocytic (>100): B12/folate deficiency, alcohol, hypothyroidism. PhenoAge component (coefficient 0.0268).
text_chunk: MCV (80-100 fL) is a PhenoAge biological age component (coefficient 0.0268). Microcytic anemia (<80 fL) suggests iron deficiency or thalassemia. Macrocytic (>100 fL) suggests B12/folate deficiency, often worsened by MTHFR C677T variants.

name: MCH
clinical_significance: Mean corpuscular hemoglobin. Amount of hemoglobin per red blood cell. Low in iron deficiency, thalassemia. High in macrocytic anemias.
text_chunk: MCH (27-33 pg) measures average hemoglobin mass per RBC. Parallels MCV in classification of anemias. Low MCH with low MCV is classic for iron deficiency anemia.

name: MCHC
clinical_significance: Mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration. Low in iron deficiency. Elevated in spherocytosis, severe dehydration.
text_chunk: MCHC (32-36 g/dL) is the average concentration of hemoglobin in RBCs. Low MCHC confirms hypochromic anemia. Elevated MCHC (>36) is characteristic of hereditary spherocytosis.

name: RDW
clinical_significance: Red cell distribution width. Measures variation in RBC size. Elevated in iron deficiency, mixed anemias, early nutritional deficiency. PhenoAge component (coefficient 0.0952).
text_chunk: RDW (11.5-14.5%) is a PhenoAge biological age component (coefficient 0.0952). Elevated RDW reflects anisocytosis and is an independent predictor of mortality. Higher RDW accelerates biological age calculation.

name: Platelets
clinical_significance: Platelet count. Low in ITP, liver cirrhosis, bone marrow failure, DIC. High in reactive thrombocytosis, myeloproliferative disorders.
text_chunk: Platelets (150-400 x10^3/µL). Thrombocytopenia (<150) may indicate liver disease, ITP, or bone marrow suppression. Thrombocytosis (>400) is often reactive to inflammation or iron deficiency.

name: Neutrophils
clinical_significance: Most abundant WBC type. Elevated in bacterial infection, stress, corticosteroid use. Low (neutropenia <1.5) increases infection risk.
text_chunk: Neutrophils (1.8-7.7 x10^3/µL) are the primary defense against bacterial infection. Neutropenia (<1.5) significantly increases infection risk. DARC-null variant (common in African ancestry) causes benign ethnic neutropenia.

name: Lymphocytes
clinical_significance: Adaptive immune cells. Elevated in viral infections, CLL. Low in HIV, immunosuppression, corticosteroid use.
text_chunk: Lymphocytes (1.0-4.8 x10^3/µL) mediate adaptive immunity. Lymphocytosis suggests viral infection or CLL. Lymphopenia (<1.0) seen in HIV, autoimmune disease, and corticosteroid use.

name: Monocytes
clinical_significance: Innate immune phagocytes. Elevated in chronic infections, autoimmune disease, CMML. Part of monocyte-to-HDL ratio (inflammatory marker).
text_chunk: Monocytes (0.2-0.8 x10^3/µL) are innate immune phagocytes. Monocytosis suggests chronic infection, autoimmune disease, or CMML. Monocyte-to-HDL ratio is an emerging cardiovascular inflammatory biomarker.

name: Eosinophils
clinical_significance: Elevated in allergic conditions, parasitic infections, eosinophilic esophagitis, drug reactions, hypereosinophilic syndrome.
text_chunk: Eosinophils (0.0-0.5 x10^3/µL). Eosinophilia (>0.5) suggests allergic disease, parasitic infection, or drug reaction. Marked eosinophilia (>1.5) warrants evaluation for hypereosinophilic syndrome.

name: Basophils
clinical_significance: Least common WBC. Elevated in allergic reactions, CML, myeloproliferative disorders. Mediates IgE-dependent allergic responses.
text_chunk: Basophils (0.0-0.1 x10^3/µL) are the rarest circulating WBC. Basophilia may indicate CML or myeloproliferative neoplasm. They release histamine and play a role in allergic inflammation.

name: Lymphocyte %
clinical_significance: Percentage of WBCs that are lymphocytes. PhenoAge component (protective coefficient -0.0120). Low lymphocyte percentage associated with immunosenescence and accelerated aging.
text_chunk: Lymphocyte % (20-40%) is a PhenoAge component with protective coefficient -0.0120. Lower lymphocyte percentage reflects immunosenescence and accelerates biological age. Values <20% in older adults associated with increased mortality.

name: Glucose
clinical_significance: Fasting blood glucose. Pre-diabetes 100-125 mg/dL, diabetes >=126 mg/dL. PhenoAge component (log-transformed, coefficient 0.00188). TCF7L2 rs7903146 TT carriers need tighter thresholds.
text_chunk: Fasting glucose (70-99 mg/dL) is a PhenoAge component (coefficient 0.00188, log-transformed). Pre-diabetes: 100-125 mg/dL. Diabetes: >=126. TCF7L2 rs7903146 TT carriers have impaired beta-cell function and should use tighter thresholds (<95 mg/dL optimal).

name: BUN
clinical_significance: Blood urea nitrogen. Reflects protein metabolism and kidney function. Elevated in renal failure, dehydration, GI bleeding, high protein intake. Low in liver disease, malnutrition.
text_chunk: BUN (7-20 mg/dL) reflects renal function and protein catabolism. BUN/Creatinine ratio >20 suggests pre-renal azotemia (dehydration, heart failure). Low BUN may indicate liver disease or low protein intake.

name: Creatinine
clinical_significance: Kidney filtration marker. Elevated in renal impairment. Used to calculate eGFR. PhenoAge component (log-transformed, coefficient 0.0095). Muscle mass affects levels.
text_chunk: Creatinine (0.7-1.3 mg/dL male, 0.6-1.1 female) is a PhenoAge component (coefficient 0.0095, log-transformed). Primary marker of kidney function used to calculate eGFR. Higher creatinine accelerates biological age. Muscle mass and diet affect levels independently of kidney function.

name: Sodium
clinical_significance: Primary extracellular cation. Hyponatremia (<136): SIADH, diuretics, heart failure. Hypernatremia (>145): dehydration, diabetes insipidus.
text_chunk: Sodium (136-145 mEq/L) regulates fluid balance and blood pressure. Hyponatremia (<136) is the most common electrolyte disorder, often from SIADH, diuretics, or heart failure. Hypernatremia (>145) usually indicates free water deficit.

name: Potassium
clinical_significance: Critical for cardiac rhythm. Hypokalemia (<3.5): arrhythmias, muscle weakness. Hyperkalemia (>5.0): cardiac arrest risk. ACE inhibitors, ARBs, and spironolactone increase levels.
text_chunk: Potassium (3.5-5.0 mEq/L) is critical for cardiac conduction. Hypokalemia and hyperkalemia both cause life-threatening arrhythmias. ACE inhibitors, ARBs, and potassium-sparing diuretics can elevate potassium. Renal impairment impairs potassium excretion.

name: Chloride
clinical_significance: Major extracellular anion. Helps assess acid-base status. Low in metabolic alkalosis (vomiting). High in metabolic acidosis, dehydration.
text_chunk: Chloride (98-106 mEq/L) is the major extracellular anion. Hypochloremia suggests metabolic alkalosis (vomiting, NG suction). Hyperchloremia indicates non-anion gap metabolic acidosis. Useful for calculating anion gap (Na - Cl - CO2).

name: CO2
clinical_significance: Bicarbonate (total CO2). Low in metabolic acidosis (DKA, renal tubular acidosis, diarrhea). High in metabolic alkalosis (vomiting, diuretics).
text_chunk: CO2/Bicarbonate (23-29 mEq/L) reflects acid-base balance. Low bicarbonate (<23) indicates metabolic acidosis. High bicarbonate (>29) indicates metabolic alkalosis. Part of the anion gap calculation for differential diagnosis of acidosis.

name: Calcium
clinical_significance: Essential for bone, nerve, and muscle function. Hypercalcemia: hyperparathyroidism, malignancy. Hypocalcemia: vitamin D deficiency, hypoparathyroidism. Always correct for albumin.
text_chunk: Calcium (8.5-10.5 mg/dL) must be corrected for albumin: corrected Ca = measured Ca + 0.8 x (4.0 - albumin). Hypercalcemia most commonly from primary hyperparathyroidism or malignancy. VDR polymorphisms affect calcium absorption and vitamin D metabolism.

name: Total Protein
clinical_significance: Sum of albumin and globulins. Low in malnutrition, nephrotic syndrome. High in multiple myeloma, chronic infection, dehydration.
text_chunk: Total Protein (6.0-8.3 g/dL) includes albumin and globulins. A/G ratio <1.0 suggests chronic infection, autoimmune disease, or multiple myeloma. Low total protein indicates malnutrition or protein-losing states.

name: Albumin
clinical_significance: Liver synthetic function marker. Low albumin indicates chronic liver disease, malnutrition, or nephrotic syndrome. PhenoAge component (protective coefficient -0.0336).
text_chunk: Albumin (3.5-5.0 g/dL) is a negative acute phase reactant and PhenoAge biological age component (protective coefficient -0.0336). Low levels (<3.5) associated with increased mortality risk and accelerated biological aging. Higher albumin is protective.

name: Bilirubin Total
clinical_significance: Heme metabolism product. Elevated in liver disease, hemolysis, Gilbert syndrome (benign, UGT1A1 variant). Mildly elevated bilirubin may be cardioprotective (antioxidant).
text_chunk: Total Bilirubin (0.1-1.2 mg/dL). Gilbert syndrome (UGT1A1 *28/*28) causes benign unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia (1.5-3.0 mg/dL). Mildly elevated bilirubin is cardioprotective due to antioxidant properties. Conjugated >0.3 mg/dL suggests hepatobiliary disease.

name: Alkaline Phosphatase
clinical_significance: Elevated in cholestasis, bone disease, pregnancy. PhenoAge component (log-transformed, coefficient 0.0203). Isoenzymes differentiate liver vs bone origin.
text_chunk: Alkaline Phosphatase (44-147 U/L) is a PhenoAge component (coefficient 0.0203, log-transformed). Elevated in cholestatic liver disease and bone disorders (Paget's, osteomalacia). GGT co-elevation confirms hepatic origin. Higher ALP accelerates biological age.

name: AST
clinical_significance: Aspartate aminotransferase. Found in liver, heart, muscle. Elevated in hepatitis, MI, rhabdomyolysis. AST/ALT ratio >2 suggests alcoholic liver disease.
text_chunk: AST (10-40 U/L) is found in liver, heart, and muscle. AST/ALT ratio >2 suggests alcoholic hepatitis. PNPLA3 rs738409 GG carriers may have elevated AST from NAFLD. Part of FIB-4 index calculation for liver fibrosis staging.

name: ALT
clinical_significance: Alanine aminotransferase. Most specific liver enzyme. Elevated in hepatitis, NAFLD, drug toxicity. PNPLA3 GG carriers have genetically elevated ALT baseline.
text_chunk: ALT (7-56 U/L) is the most liver-specific aminotransferase. PNPLA3 rs738409 GG carriers have ~10-15 U/L higher baseline ALT and should use adjusted thresholds. HSD17B13 loss-of-function rs72613567:TA is protective, lowering ALT by ~4 U/L.

name: Total Cholesterol
clinical_significance: Sum of LDL, HDL, VLDL. Desirable <200, borderline 200-239, high >=240. APOE genotype significantly affects cholesterol levels and statin response.
text_chunk: Total Cholesterol (desirable <200 mg/dL). APOE4 carriers have ~10-15 mg/dL higher total cholesterol. PCSK9 loss-of-function variants confer lifelong lower LDL and ~88% reduced CHD risk. Optimal total cholesterol for longevity is 160-200 mg/dL.

name: LDL-C
clinical_significance: Primary atherogenic lipoprotein. Optimal <100, near-optimal 100-129, high >=160. APOE4 carriers have higher LDL. PCSK9 inhibitors reduce LDL 50-60%.
text_chunk: LDL-C (optimal <100 mg/dL, <70 for high CV risk). APOE4/4 genotype increases LDL ~30 mg/dL. PCSK9 loss-of-function (rs11591147 T allele) lowers lifetime LDL ~15-28%. ApoB is a more accurate measure of atherogenic particle number than calculated LDL.

name: HDL-C
clinical_significance: Reverse cholesterol transport. Low HDL (<40 male, <50 female) is a CV risk factor. Very high HDL (>90) may not be additionally protective (U-shaped curve).
text_chunk: HDL-C (>40 mg/dL male, >50 female). Low HDL is an independent CV risk factor. CETP variants affect HDL levels. Very high HDL (>90) shows diminishing returns for cardioprotection. HDL functionality (efflux capacity) may matter more than absolute level.

name: Triglycerides
clinical_significance: Fasting triglycerides. Optimal <100, normal <150, high 200-499, very high >=500 (pancreatitis risk). GCKR rs780094 TT and APOE4 raise triglycerides.
text_chunk: Triglycerides (optimal <100 mg/dL, normal <150). Elevated triglycerides are an independent CV risk factor. GCKR rs780094 TT carriers have genetically higher triglycerides. Very high TG (>500) increases pancreatitis risk. Omega-3 fatty acids (EPA) reduce TG 20-30%.

name: VLDL
clinical_significance: Very low-density lipoprotein. Calculated as triglycerides/5. Carries triglycerides from liver. Elevated in metabolic syndrome, insulin resistance.
text_chunk: VLDL (2-30 mg/dL) is estimated as triglycerides/5. Elevated VLDL reflects hepatic triglyceride overproduction, common in insulin resistance and metabolic syndrome. VLDL remnants are atherogenic.

name: ApoB
clinical_significance: Apolipoprotein B. One ApoB per atherogenic particle (LDL, VLDL, IDL, Lp(a)). Better predictor of CV risk than LDL-C. Optimal <90, high risk <80.
text_chunk: ApoB (optimal <90 mg/dL, <80 for high CV risk). ApoB counts all atherogenic particles and is superior to LDL-C for CV risk prediction. Discordance between LDL-C and ApoB is common in metabolic syndrome.

name: Lp(a)
clinical_significance: Lipoprotein(a). Genetically determined, 90% by LPA gene. >75 nmol/L (>30 mg/dL) is elevated CV risk. Not reducible by lifestyle. PCSK9i reduces ~20-30%.
text_chunk: Lp(a) (desirable <75 nmol/L or <30 mg/dL). >90% genetically determined by LPA gene kringle IV type 2 repeats. Elevated Lp(a) is an independent, causal CV risk factor. Resistant to lifestyle modification. PCSK9 inhibitors reduce 20-30%. Novel antisense therapies (pelacarsen) in trials.

name: Omega-3 Index
clinical_significance: EPA+DHA as percentage of RBC membrane fatty acids. Optimal >8%. <4% high CV risk zone. FADS1 rs174546 CC carriers have impaired conversion from ALA.
text_chunk: Omega-3 Index (optimal 8-12%). Measures EPA+DHA in RBC membranes, reflecting 120-day intake. <4% is high risk zone. FADS1 rs174546 CC carriers have reduced delta-5 desaturase activity and need preformed EPA/DHA (fish oil) rather than plant-based ALA.

name: TSH
clinical_significance: Thyroid-stimulating hormone. Primary thyroid screen. High in hypothyroidism, low in hyperthyroidism. DIO2 Thr92Ala carriers may need higher TSH for adequate T3.
text_chunk: TSH (0.4-4.0 mIU/L) is the primary thyroid screening test. DIO2 rs225014 AA (Thr92Ala) carriers have impaired T4-to-T3 conversion and may be symptomatic with 'normal' TSH. Optimal TSH for DIO2 AA carriers may be 0.5-1.5 mIU/L with combination T4/T3 therapy.

name: Free T4
clinical_significance: Active thyroid hormone precursor. Low in hypothyroidism. High in hyperthyroidism, excessive supplementation. T4 is converted to active T3 by deiodinases.
text_chunk: Free T4 (0.8-1.8 ng/dL) is the unbound, bioavailable form of thyroxine. T4 is converted to active T3 by type 1 and type 2 deiodinases (DIO1, DIO2). DIO2 Thr92Ala variant impairs this conversion, potentially requiring combination T4/T3 therapy.

name: Free T3
clinical_significance: Most active thyroid hormone. DIO2 Thr92Ala carriers often have low-normal Free T3 despite adequate TSH. Low T3 syndrome in critical illness.
text_chunk: Free T3 (2.3-4.2 pg/mL) is the most metabolically active thyroid hormone. DIO2 rs225014 AA carriers may have low-normal Free T3 (2.3-2.8) with persistent hypothyroid symptoms. Free T3/Free T4 ratio <0.25 suggests impaired T4-to-T3 conversion.

name: Thyroid Peroxidase Ab
clinical_significance: TPO antibodies. Elevated in Hashimoto thyroiditis, Graves disease. Positive TPO Ab with normal TSH predicts future hypothyroidism (2-4% per year conversion).
text_chunk: TPO Antibodies (normal <35 IU/mL). Present in 90% of Hashimoto thyroiditis and 70% of Graves disease. Positive TPO Ab with normal TSH confers 2-4% annual risk of developing hypothyroidism. Selenium supplementation (200 mcg/day) may reduce TPO Ab titers.

name: Serum Iron
clinical_significance: Circulating iron. Low in iron deficiency, chronic disease anemia. High in hemochromatosis, iron overload. Diurnal variation (highest AM). Must interpret with ferritin, TIBC.
text_chunk: Serum Iron (60-170 mcg/dL) has significant diurnal variation (highest in morning). Must be interpreted alongside ferritin and TIBC. HFE C282Y homozygotes have increased iron absorption leading to elevated serum iron.

name: Ferritin
clinical_significance: Iron storage protein. Best single test for iron deficiency (<20). Elevated in hemochromatosis, inflammation (acute phase reactant). HFE C282Y carriers need monitoring. GrimAge surrogate marker.
text_chunk: Ferritin (20-250 ng/mL male, 20-120 female) is the best single test for iron deficiency. HFE C282Y homozygotes should maintain ferritin <300 ng/mL via therapeutic phlebotomy. Ferritin is also an acute phase reactant; elevated CRP may falsely elevate ferritin. Optimal longevity range: 40-100 ng/mL.

name: TIBC
clinical_significance: Total iron-binding capacity. High in iron deficiency (compensatory). Low in iron overload, chronic disease. Inversely related to iron stores.
text_chunk: TIBC (250-370 mcg/dL) reflects transferrin availability. Elevated TIBC with low ferritin confirms iron deficiency anemia. Low TIBC with high ferritin suggests iron overload or anemia of chronic disease.

name: Transferrin Saturation
clinical_significance: Iron/TIBC ratio. >45% suggests iron overload. HFE C282Y screening cutoff. <20% suggests iron deficiency. Key hemochromatosis screening test.
text_chunk: Transferrin Saturation (20-50%). >45% is the primary screening cutoff for hereditary hemochromatosis (HFE C282Y). HFE C282Y homozygotes typically have TSAT >60%. <20% with low ferritin confirms iron deficiency. TSAT >45% warrants HFE genotyping.

name: Transferrin
clinical_significance: Iron transport protein synthesized by liver. High in iron deficiency. Low in chronic disease, liver failure, malnutrition. Negative acute phase reactant.
text_chunk: Transferrin (200-360 mg/dL) is the primary iron transport protein. Elevated in iron deficiency as the body upregulates production. Decreased as a negative acute phase reactant during inflammation.

name: GGT
clinical_significance: Gamma-glutamyl transferase. Most sensitive liver enzyme for alcohol and biliary disease. Elevated with ALP confirms hepatic origin. GrimAge surrogate marker.
text_chunk: GGT (0-65 U/L) is the most sensitive marker for alcohol-related liver damage and cholestasis. Co-elevation with ALP confirms hepatobiliary origin. GGT is independently associated with cardiovascular mortality. Elevated GGT accelerates GrimAge biological age.

name: LDH
clinical_significance: Lactate dehydrogenase. Non-specific marker of tissue damage. Elevated in hemolysis, liver disease, MI, cancer, pulmonary embolism.
text_chunk: LDH (140-280 U/L) is a non-specific marker present in all tissues. Markedly elevated in hemolytic anemia, lymphoma, and tissue infarction. LDH isoenzymes help localize the source: LDH-1 (heart), LDH-5 (liver/muscle).

name: Direct Bilirubin
clinical_significance: Conjugated bilirubin. Elevated in hepatocellular disease, biliary obstruction, Dubin-Johnson syndrome. Direct/Total ratio >50% suggests obstructive cause.
text_chunk: Direct Bilirubin (0.0-0.3 mg/dL) is the conjugated, water-soluble form. Elevated direct bilirubin (>0.3 mg/dL or >50% of total) indicates hepatocellular damage or biliary obstruction.

name: FIB-4 Index
clinical_significance: Non-invasive liver fibrosis score: (Age x AST) / (Platelets x sqrt(ALT)). <1.3 excludes advanced fibrosis. >2.67 suggests advanced fibrosis. Critical for PNPLA3 GG carriers.
text_chunk: FIB-4 Index (<1.3 excludes fibrosis, >2.67 suggests advanced fibrosis). Calculated: (Age x AST) / (Platelets x sqrt(ALT)). PNPLA3 rs738409 GG carriers should be monitored with FIB-4 annually. Values 1.3-2.67 are indeterminate and warrant FibroScan or liver biopsy.

name: hs-CRP
clinical_significance: High-sensitivity C-reactive protein. CV risk: <1 low, 1-3 moderate, >3 high. PhenoAge component (log-transformed, coefficient 0.0167). IL6 rs1800795 GG carriers have higher baseline CRP.
text_chunk: hs-CRP (optimal <1.0 mg/L) is a PhenoAge component (coefficient 0.0167, log-transformed). CV risk stratification: <1 low, 1-3 moderate, >3 high risk (JUPITER trial). CRP rs1800947 and IL6 rs1800795 affect baseline CRP levels.

name: ESR
clinical_significance: Erythrocyte sedimentation rate. Non-specific inflammation marker. Elevated in infection, autoimmune disease, malignancy. Age-adjusted: upper limit = age/2 (male).
text_chunk: ESR (0-20 mm/hr) is a non-specific inflammatory marker. Age-adjusted upper normal: age/2 for males, (age+10)/2 for females. Very high ESR (>100) suggests multiple myeloma, temporal arteritis, or severe infection.

name: IL-6
clinical_significance: Interleukin-6. Pro-inflammatory cytokine. Drives CRP production. Elevated in sepsis, autoimmune disease, CV disease. IL6 rs1800795 GG carriers have higher baseline. GrimAge component.
text_chunk: IL-6 (0-7 pg/mL) is a key pro-inflammatory cytokine that drives hepatic CRP production. IL6 rs1800795 GG carriers have genetically higher IL-6 levels. Elevated IL-6 is a GrimAge biological aging component.

name: TNF-alpha
clinical_significance: Tumor necrosis factor alpha. Master inflammatory cytokine. Elevated in autoimmune disease, sepsis, obesity. TNF inhibitors used in RA, Crohn's, psoriasis.
text_chunk: TNF-alpha (0-8.1 pg/mL) is a master pro-inflammatory cytokine produced by macrophages. Chronically elevated in obesity, autoimmune disease, and metabolic syndrome. TNF-alpha inhibitors (infliximab, adalimumab) revolutionized treatment of RA, IBD, and psoriasis.

name: Fibrinogen
clinical_significance: Coagulation factor and acute phase reactant. Elevated in inflammation, CV risk. Low in DIC, liver failure. GrimAge surrogate marker.
text_chunk: Fibrinogen (200-400 mg/dL) is both a coagulation factor and acute phase reactant. Elevated fibrinogen is an independent CV risk factor. GrimAge biological age surrogate - higher fibrinogen accelerates epigenetic aging.

name: Homocysteine
clinical_significance: Methionine metabolism intermediate. Elevated in MTHFR C677T (especially TT), B12/folate deficiency. CV risk factor. Optimal <10 umol/L.
text_chunk: Homocysteine (optimal <10 umol/L). MTHFR rs1801133 TT homozygotes have ~25% higher homocysteine due to impaired folate metabolism. Treatment: L-methylfolate (not folic acid for MTHFR TT), B12, B6. MTRR A66G also elevates homocysteine.

name: HbA1c
clinical_significance: Glycated hemoglobin reflecting 90-day average glucose. Pre-diabetes 5.7-6.4%, diabetes >=6.5%. TCF7L2 TT carriers warrant tighter targets. Hemoglobin variants affect accuracy.
text_chunk: HbA1c (normal <5.7%, pre-diabetes 5.7-6.4%, diabetes >=6.5%). Reflects 90-day average glucose. TCF7L2 rs7903146 TT carriers should aim for HbA1c <5.5%. Hemoglobin variants (HbS, HbC, HbE) and iron deficiency affect HbA1c accuracy.

name: Fasting Insulin
clinical_significance: Early insulin resistance marker. >15 suggests significant insulin resistance. Used in HOMA-IR calculation. Rises years before glucose in T2D trajectory.
text_chunk: Fasting Insulin (optimal 2.6-11.0 uIU/mL). Elevated fasting insulin (>15) is an early marker of insulin resistance, appearing years before glucose abnormalities. Optimal for metabolic health: 3-8 uIU/mL. HOMA-IR calculation: (glucose x insulin) / 405.

name: HOMA-IR
clinical_significance: Homeostatic Model Assessment of Insulin Resistance. >2.5 suggests insulin resistance, >3.0 strongly suggests IR. Calculated: (fasting glucose x fasting insulin) / 405.
text_chunk: HOMA-IR (optimal <1.4, insulin resistance >2.5). Calculated: (fasting glucose mg/dL x fasting insulin uIU/mL) / 405. HOMA-IR >3.0 strongly suggests insulin resistance. DPP trial showed lifestyle intervention reduces HOMA-IR significantly.

name: C-Peptide
clinical_significance: Co-secreted with insulin. Differentiates type 1 (low) from type 2 (normal/high) diabetes. Not affected by exogenous insulin.
text_chunk: C-Peptide (0.8-3.1 ng/mL) is co-secreted with insulin in equimolar amounts. Low C-Peptide (<0.6) indicates beta-cell failure (Type 1 or late Type 2). Normal/elevated C-Peptide with hyperglycemia confirms insulin resistance.

name: Testosterone Total
clinical_significance: Primary male sex hormone. Low testosterone (<300): hypogonadism symptoms, increased CV risk, metabolic syndrome. SHBG affects bioavailable fraction.
text_chunk: Total Testosterone (300-1000 ng/dL male, 15-70 female). Symptomatic hypogonadism typically <300 ng/dL. SHBG elevation reduces bioavailable testosterone. Free testosterone or calculated bioavailable testosterone more clinically relevant.

name: Testosterone Free
clinical_significance: Unbound, bioactive testosterone. More clinically relevant than total testosterone. Calculated from total T, SHBG, and albumin (Vermeulen equation).
text_chunk: Free Testosterone (9-30 pg/mL male) is the bioavailable fraction not bound to SHBG or albumin. More clinically relevant than total T, especially when SHBG is abnormal.

name: Estradiol
clinical_significance: Primary estrogen. In males, optimal 20-35 pg/mL. Too low: bone loss, CV risk. Too high: gynecomastia. Aromatase converts testosterone to estradiol.
text_chunk: Estradiol (male 10-40 pg/mL). In males, optimal 20-35 pg/mL. Low estradiol (<20) increases osteoporosis and CV risk. High estradiol (>40) causes gynecomastia. CYP19A1 (aromatase) variants affect conversion rate.

name: DHEA-S
clinical_significance: Adrenal androgen precursor. Declines ~2% per year after age 25. Low levels associated with aging, adrenal insufficiency. Marker of adrenal reserve.
text_chunk: DHEA-S (age-dependent, 80-560 mcg/dL) is the most abundant circulating steroid and a marker of adrenal reserve. Declines ~2% annually from peak at age 20-25.

name: Cortisol AM
clinical_significance: Morning cortisol. Adrenal function marker. Low in Addison disease. High in Cushing syndrome, chronic stress. Diurnal pattern: highest 6-8 AM.
text_chunk: AM Cortisol (6-18 mcg/dL at 8 AM). <3 strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency. >18 essentially excludes adrenal insufficiency. Cushing syndrome: elevated with loss of diurnal variation.

name: IGF-1
clinical_significance: Insulin-like growth factor 1. Mediates growth hormone effects. Age-dependent decline. Low: GH deficiency. High: acromegaly. Longevity data suggests lower-normal may be optimal.
text_chunk: IGF-1 (100-300 ng/mL, age-dependent). Mediates GH anabolic effects. Low IGF-1 (<100) suggests GH deficiency. Longevity research suggests lower-normal IGF-1 (100-150) may optimize healthspan.

name: SHBG
clinical_significance: Sex hormone-binding globulin. Binds testosterone and estradiol. High SHBG reduces free hormone levels. Elevated by aging, hyperthyroidism, liver disease.
text_chunk: SHBG (10-57 nmol/L male, 18-144 female). High SHBG reduces bioavailable testosterone and estradiol. Elevated in aging, hyperthyroidism, liver cirrhosis. Decreased in obesity, insulin resistance.

name: Prolactin
clinical_significance: Pituitary hormone. Elevated by pituitary adenoma, medications (antipsychotics, SSRIs), hypothyroidism. Causes galactorrhea, hypogonadism.
text_chunk: Prolactin (2-18 ng/mL male, 2-29 female). Mild elevation (25-100) commonly from medications (antipsychotics, metoclopramide, SSRIs). >200 ng/mL strongly suggests prolactinoma.

name: Vitamin D 25-OH
clinical_significance: 25-hydroxyvitamin D. Deficient <20, insufficient 20-29, sufficient 30-100. VDR rs2228570 variants affect receptor sensitivity. Optimal 40-60 ng/mL.
text_chunk: Vitamin D 25-OH (sufficient 30-100 ng/mL, optimal 40-60). Deficient <20 ng/mL, insufficient 20-29. VDR rs2228570 CC carriers may need higher vitamin D levels for optimal receptor activation (target 50-60).

name: Vitamin B12
clinical_significance: Cobalamin. Deficiency causes megaloblastic anemia, neuropathy, cognitive decline. MTRR rs1801394 AG/GG carriers have impaired B12 metabolism.
text_chunk: Vitamin B12 (200-900 pg/mL, optimal >400). MTRR rs1801394 A66G variant impairs methionine synthase reductase, requiring higher B12 levels. Methylcobalamin preferred over cyanocobalamin for MTRR variant carriers.

name: Folate
clinical_significance: Vitamin B9. MTHFR C677T TT carriers have impaired folate metabolism and should use L-methylfolate instead of folic acid.
text_chunk: Folate (3-20 ng/mL, optimal >10). MTHFR rs1801133 TT homozygotes have ~70% reduced enzyme activity and should use L-methylfolate (5-MTHF) instead of folic acid. Folic acid can accumulate unmetabolized in MTHFR TT carriers.

name: Vitamin A
clinical_significance: Retinol. Deficiency causes night blindness, immune dysfunction. Toxicity from excess supplementation causes liver damage. Fat-soluble.
text_chunk: Vitamin A/Retinol (38-98 mcg/dL). BCMO1 variants impair beta-carotene to retinol conversion, making preformed retinol necessary. Toxicity risk with >10,000 IU/day supplementation.

name: Vitamin E
clinical_significance: Alpha-tocopherol. Antioxidant. Deficiency rare, causes peripheral neuropathy, ataxia. High-dose supplementation (>400 IU/day) may increase mortality.
text_chunk: Vitamin E (5.5-17.0 mg/L). Primary fat-soluble antioxidant. Deficiency causes progressive neuropathy and ataxia. SELECT trial showed no cancer benefit with high-dose supplementation.

name: Vitamin K
clinical_significance: Essential for coagulation factors II, VII, IX, X and osteocalcin. K1 from greens, K2 from fermented foods. VKORC1 variants affect warfarin dosing.
text_chunk: Vitamin K (0.2-3.2 ng/mL). K1 is essential for clotting factor synthesis. K2 (MK-7) important for calcium metabolism. VKORC1 AA genotype has increased vitamin K sensitivity - important for warfarin dosing.

name: Vitamin B6
clinical_significance: Pyridoxal 5-phosphate (PLP). Cofactor in >150 enzymatic reactions. Deficiency causes peripheral neuropathy, anemia. Important for homocysteine metabolism.
text_chunk: Vitamin B6/PLP (5-50 ng/mL). Cofactor for >150 enzymatic reactions including transamination and homocysteine metabolism. Deficiency is common (10-25% of adults) and causes peripheral neuropathy.

name: Vitamin B1
clinical_significance: Thiamine. Deficiency causes Wernicke encephalopathy, beriberi, peripheral neuropathy. At risk: alcoholics, bariatric surgery patients.
text_chunk: Vitamin B1/Thiamine (70-180 nmol/L). Deficiency causes Wernicke-Korsakoff syndrome and beriberi. High-risk groups: alcohol use disorder, bariatric surgery, heart failure on furosemide.

name: Magnesium
clinical_significance: Essential mineral. Deficiency common (>50% of population). Causes muscle cramps, arrhythmias, insulin resistance. Glycinate form best absorbed.
text_chunk: Magnesium (1.7-2.2 mg/dL serum, optimal >2.0). Serum magnesium reflects only 1% of body stores; RBC magnesium is more accurate. Deficiency causes muscle cramps, arrhythmias, insulin resistance. Magnesium glycinate has superior absorption.

name: Zinc
clinical_significance: Essential trace mineral for immune function, wound healing, testosterone synthesis, >300 enzymes. Competes with copper for absorption.
text_chunk: Zinc (60-120 mcg/dL). Essential for >300 enzymatic reactions, immune function, and testosterone synthesis. SLC30A8 variants affect zinc transport in pancreatic beta-cells.

name: Selenium
clinical_significance: Essential for thyroid function (deiodinases), glutathione peroxidase. Deficiency impairs T4-to-T3 conversion. 200 mcg/day may reduce TPO antibodies.
text_chunk: Selenium (70-150 mcg/L, optimal 100-130). Essential cofactor for deiodinases (T4-to-T3 conversion) and glutathione peroxidase. 200 mcg/day selenomethionine reduces TPO antibodies in Hashimoto thyroiditis.

name: Copper
clinical_significance: Essential trace element. Ceruloplasmin carries 95%. Low in Wilson disease. Cu/Zn ratio >1.5 suggests inflammation.
text_chunk: Copper (70-155 mcg/dL). Copper/Zinc ratio >1.5 is an inflammatory marker. Wilson disease: low ceruloplasmin, low total copper, high free copper. ATP7B mutations cause Wilson disease.

name: Iodine
clinical_significance: Essential for thyroid hormone synthesis. Urinary iodine reflects recent intake. Deficiency causes goiter, hypothyroidism.
text_chunk: Urinary Iodine (100-300 mcg/L adequate). Iodine is essential for T3/T4 synthesis. Excess iodine (>1000 mcg/day) can trigger Hashimoto thyroiditis flare in susceptible individuals.

name: Cystatin C
clinical_significance: Superior GFR marker unaffected by muscle mass, diet, or sex. GrimAge component. Better than creatinine in elderly.
text_chunk: Cystatin C (0.6-1.0 mg/L) is a GrimAge biological age component. Superior to creatinine for GFR estimation as it is unaffected by muscle mass, diet, age, or sex. Elevated cystatin C independently predicts cardiovascular events.

name: eGFR
clinical_significance: Estimated glomerular filtration rate. CKD staging: G1 >90, G2 60-89, G3a 45-59, G3b 30-44, G4 15-29, G5 <15.
text_chunk: eGFR (normal >90 mL/min/1.73m2). CKD staging: G1 >90, G2 60-89, G3a 45-59, G3b 30-44, G4 15-29, G5 <15. CKD-EPI 2021 race-free equation recommended. eGFR decline >5 mL/min/year is rapid progression.

name: Uric Acid
clinical_significance: Purine metabolism end product. High in gout (>6.8 crystallization threshold), metabolic syndrome. Target <6.0 in gout.
text_chunk: Uric Acid (3.5-7.2 mg/dL male, 2.6-6.0 female). Monosodium urate crystallization threshold is 6.8 mg/dL. SLC2A9 and ABCG2 variants affect urate handling. Hyperuricemia independently associated with CV disease.

name: Microalbumin/Creatinine Ratio
clinical_significance: Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio. First sign of diabetic nephropathy. Normal <30, microalbuminuria 30-300, macroalbuminuria >300.
text_chunk: Urine Albumin/Creatinine Ratio (normal <30 mg/g, microalbuminuria 30-300, macroalbuminuria >300). Earliest marker of diabetic nephropathy. Also reflects systemic endothelial dysfunction and is an independent CV risk factor.

name: BNP
clinical_significance: B-type natriuretic peptide. Heart failure biomarker. BNP >400 strongly suggests HF. Obesity falsely lowers BNP.
text_chunk: BNP (<100 pg/mL). Primary heart failure biomarker. BNP >400 strongly suggests heart failure. Obesity reduces BNP levels (falsely reassuring). Renal impairment elevates BNP.

name: NT-proBNP
clinical_significance: N-terminal pro-B-type natriuretic peptide. More stable than BNP, longer half-life. Age-dependent cutoffs for acute HF diagnosis.
text_chunk: NT-proBNP (normal <125 pg/mL age <75, <450 age >75). Preferred over BNP due to longer half-life. Age-stratified acute HF rule-in: <50 yrs >450, 50-75 yrs >900, >75 yrs >1800 pg/mL.

name: Troponin I
clinical_significance: Cardiac troponin I. Gold standard for myocardial injury. Elevated in MI, myocarditis, PE, renal failure.
text_chunk: Troponin I (normal <0.04 ng/mL). Gold standard biomarker for acute myocardial injury. Serial measurements at 0, 3, 6 hours for MI diagnosis. Also elevated in myocarditis, PE, severe sepsis.

name: hs-Troponin T
clinical_significance: High-sensitivity troponin T. Detects subclinical myocardial injury. >14 ng/L: myocardial injury. Chronic elevation predicts CV mortality.
text_chunk: hs-Troponin T (normal <14 ng/L). High-sensitivity assay detects subclinical myocardial injury. Delta change >50% in 1-2 hours diagnoses acute MI. Chronically elevated hs-TnT (14-50) independently predicts CV mortality.

name: Galectin-3
clinical_significance: Cardiac fibrosis biomarker. Elevated in heart failure with fibrotic remodeling. >25.9 ng/mL associated with high risk of HF mortality.
text_chunk: Galectin-3 (<17.8 ng/mL). Marker of cardiac fibrosis and remodeling. Elevated galectin-3 in heart failure predicts worse outcomes independent of BNP. >25.9 ng/mL associated with 4-year mortality >50% in HF patients.

name: GDF-15
clinical_significance: Growth differentiation factor 15. Stress-responsive cytokine. GrimAge surrogate. Strong mortality predictor. Elevated in aging, cancer, CV disease.
text_chunk: GDF-15 (200-1200 pg/mL, age-dependent). Key GrimAge biological age component and one of the strongest mortality predictors. Reflects mitochondrial stress, inflammation, and cellular senescence. Levels >1800 pg/mL associated with >3x mortality risk.

name: Leptin
clinical_significance: Adipokine reflecting body fat. GrimAge surrogate. Leptin resistance in obesity. Low in lipodystrophy.
text_chunk: Leptin (1-25 ng/mL, BMI-dependent). GrimAge biological age component. Leptin resistance (elevated leptin despite obesity) drives metabolic dysfunction. FTO rs9939609 AA carriers have higher leptin. Optimal for longevity: 5-15 ng/mL.

name: PAI-1
clinical_significance: Plasminogen activator inhibitor-1. GrimAge surrogate. Elevated in obesity, metabolic syndrome. SERPINE1 loss-of-function associated with longevity.
text_chunk: PAI-1 (5-40 ng/mL). Key GrimAge component. Inhibits fibrinolysis, promoting thrombosis. SERPINE1 loss-of-function variant in Berne Amish associated with 10-year longer telomeres and reduced metabolic disease.

name: TIMP-1
clinical_significance: Tissue inhibitor of metalloproteinase-1. GrimAge surrogate. Regulates extracellular matrix remodeling. Elevated in fibrosis and cancer.
text_chunk: TIMP-1 (100-250 ng/mL). GrimAge biological age surrogate reflecting tissue remodeling and fibrosis. Elevated TIMP-1 indicates increased extracellular matrix deposition. Associated with cardiac fibrosis, liver fibrosis, and cancer progression.

name: Adrenomedullin
clinical_significance: Vasoactive peptide. GrimAge surrogate (DNAm ADM). Elevated in heart failure, sepsis, renal disease. Reflects vascular aging.
text_chunk: Adrenomedullin (10-50 pg/mL). GrimAge surrogate marker (DNAm ADM). Vasoactive peptide produced by endothelial cells. Elevated in heart failure, sepsis, and CKD. Mid-regional pro-adrenomedullin (MR-proADM) is the stable clinical assay.

name: Klotho
clinical_significance: Anti-aging protein. Low Klotho associated with accelerated aging, CKD, CV disease. Declines with age. Potential therapeutic target.
text_chunk: Soluble Klotho (300-1000 pg/mL, declines with age). Anti-aging protein. Low Klotho associated with CKD progression, cardiovascular disease, and cognitive decline. KL-VS variant affects levels. Exercise and vitamin D may increase Klotho.

name: PT/INR
clinical_significance: Prothrombin time/INR. Monitors extrinsic pathway. Therapeutic INR for warfarin: 2.0-3.0. VKORC1 and CYP2C9 genotypes affect warfarin dosing.
text_chunk: PT/INR (normal 0.8-1.2, therapeutic on warfarin 2.0-3.0). VKORC1 rs9923231 AA genotype requires ~50% lower warfarin dose. CYP2C9 *2/*3 poor metabolizers also need reduced dosing. Combined genotyping recommended before warfarin initiation.

name: aPTT
clinical_significance: Activated partial thromboplastin time. Monitors intrinsic pathway and heparin. Prolonged in hemophilia A/B, von Willebrand disease.
text_chunk: aPTT (25-35 seconds). Monitors intrinsic coagulation pathway. Prolonged in hemophilia A (Factor VIII), hemophilia B (Factor IX), von Willebrand disease. Therapeutic heparin target: 60-80 seconds.

name: D-Dimer
clinical_significance: Fibrin degradation product. High sensitivity, low specificity for VTE. Age-adjusted cutoff: age x 10 (>50 years). Normal D-Dimer excludes PE/DVT.
text_chunk: D-Dimer (<500 ng/mL, age-adjusted: age x 10 for >50). High negative predictive value for venous thromboembolism. Normal D-Dimer with low clinical probability effectively excludes PE/DVT.

name: PSA
clinical_significance: Prostate-specific antigen. Screening for prostate cancer. PSA >4.0 warrants evaluation. Free/total ratio improves specificity.
text_chunk: PSA (normal <4.0 ng/mL). Prostate cancer screening biomarker. PSA 4-10: ~25% cancer probability. Free PSA/Total <10% increases cancer probability. BRCA2 carriers should begin screening at age 40.

name: CEA
clinical_significance: Carcinoembryonic antigen. Colorectal cancer monitoring marker. Not for screening. Also elevated in smokers.
text_chunk: CEA (normal <3.0 ng/mL non-smokers, <5.0 smokers). Primary role is monitoring colorectal cancer recurrence. CEA >10 pre-surgery associated with worse prognosis. Not recommended as screening test.

name: CA-125
clinical_significance: Cancer antigen 125. Ovarian cancer biomarker. Not for general screening. Monitoring treatment response. Also elevated in endometriosis.
text_chunk: CA-125 (normal <35 U/mL). Primary use is monitoring ovarian cancer treatment. BRCA1/2 mutation carriers have elevated ovarian cancer risk. CA-125 with ultrasound used for surveillance in high-risk women.

name: AFP
clinical_significance: Alpha-fetoprotein. HCC screening in cirrhotics. Also elevated in testicular germ cell tumors. >400 ng/mL highly suggestive of HCC.
text_chunk: AFP (normal <10 ng/mL). Hepatocellular carcinoma surveillance in cirrhotic patients every 6 months. AFP >400 highly suggestive of HCC. PNPLA3 GG cirrhotic patients should have AFP surveillance.

name: CA 19-9
clinical_significance: Cancer antigen 19-9. Pancreatic cancer monitoring biomarker. Not for screening. Also elevated in biliary obstruction.
text_chunk: CA 19-9 (normal <37 U/mL). Monitoring pancreatic cancer treatment response. Lewis antigen negative individuals (FUT3 null, ~5-10%) cannot produce CA 19-9 (false negative). >1000 U/mL suggests metastatic disease.

name: ANA
clinical_significance: Antinuclear antibody. Screening for systemic autoimmune disease. 15-20% of healthy women are ANA positive.
text_chunk: ANA (negative <1:80). Positive ANA is 95% sensitive but ~50% specific for SLE at 1:80. Pattern: homogeneous (SLE), speckled (mixed CTD), centromere (limited scleroderma). 15-20% of healthy women have positive ANA.

name: Rheumatoid Factor
clinical_significance: IgM autoantibody against Fc portion of IgG. Positive in 70-80% of RA. Anti-CCP is more specific.
text_chunk: Rheumatoid Factor (normal <14 IU/mL). Positive in 70-80% of RA, also Sjogren (75%), Hep C (40%). Anti-CCP is more specific for RA (95-98%). HLA-DRB1 shared epitope increases RA susceptibility.

name: Anti-CCP
clinical_significance: Anti-cyclic citrullinated peptide. Most specific serologic marker for RA (95-98%). Can be positive years before onset.
text_chunk: Anti-CCP (normal <20 U/mL). 95-98% specific for rheumatoid arthritis. Can be positive 5-10 years before clinical RA onset. PTPN22 rs2476601 and HLA-DRB1 shared epitope are major genetic risk factors.

name: CoQ10
clinical_significance: Coenzyme Q10/Ubiquinol. Mitochondrial electron transport chain cofactor. Depleted by statins. Supplementation may reduce statin myopathy.
text_chunk: CoQ10 (0.5-1.7 mcg/mL). Essential mitochondrial cofactor for ATP production. Statin drugs deplete CoQ10 via shared mevalonate pathway. Supplementation with ubiquinol (200-400 mg/day) may reduce statin-associated myopathy.

name: Glutathione
clinical_significance: Master antioxidant. Low in oxidative stress, chronic disease, aging. NAC and GlyNAC supplementation restores levels.
text_chunk: Glutathione (200-400 umol/L whole blood). Master antioxidant. GSTM1 and GSTT1 null genotypes impair glutathione conjugation. NAC (600-1200 mg/day) or GlyNAC supplementation effectively raises glutathione. Declines ~10% per decade after age 40.

name: Lp-PLA2
clinical_significance: Lipoprotein-associated phospholipase A2. Vascular-specific inflammation marker. Elevated indicates unstable atherosclerotic plaque.
text_chunk: Lp-PLA2 (<200 ng/mL). Vascular-specific inflammatory enzyme associated with unstable atherosclerotic plaque. More specific for vascular inflammation than hs-CRP. PLA2G7 loss-of-function variant common in East Asian populations.

name: Small Dense LDL
clinical_significance: Atherogenic LDL subtype. More oxidizable, penetrates arterial wall more easily. Pattern B associated with 3x CV risk.
text_chunk: Small Dense LDL (optimal <30 mg/dL). More atherogenic than large buoyant LDL due to increased oxidizability and arterial wall penetration. Pattern B (predominant sdLDL) associated with 3x CV risk. Common in metabolic syndrome.

name: Adiponectin
clinical_significance: Anti-inflammatory adipokine. Low in obesity, metabolic syndrome. Protective against T2D and CV disease. Inversely correlated with visceral fat.
text_chunk: Adiponectin (4-20 mcg/mL). Anti-inflammatory, insulin-sensitizing adipokine. Low adiponectin is an independent risk factor for T2D and CV disease. ADIPOQ gene variants affect levels. Weight loss and TZDs increase adiponectin.

name: Haptoglobin
clinical_significance: Hemoglobin-binding protein. Low/absent in hemolytic anemia. Acute phase reactant when elevated.
text_chunk: Haptoglobin (30-200 mg/dL). Undetectable haptoglobin is the most sensitive marker for intravascular hemolysis. HP 2-2 genotype has lower baseline levels. Used with LDH and indirect bilirubin to diagnose hemolysis.

name: Reticulocyte Count
clinical_significance: Immature RBCs reflecting bone marrow erythropoietic activity. Elevated in hemolysis, blood loss. Low in aplastic anemia.
text_chunk: Reticulocyte Count (0.5-2.5%). RPI >2 suggests adequate marrow response (hemolysis, blood loss). RPI <2 in anemia suggests hypoproliferative cause (iron deficiency, B12/folate deficiency, aplastic anemia).

name: Prealbumin
clinical_significance: Transthyretin. Short half-life (2 days) makes it sensitive for acute nutritional changes. Low in malnutrition, liver disease.
text_chunk: Prealbumin/Transthyretin (20-40 mg/dL). Short half-life (2 days) vs albumin (21 days) makes it superior for monitoring acute nutritional changes. <10 mg/dL indicates severe malnutrition. TTR mutations cause familial amyloid polyneuropathy.

name: HE4
clinical_significance: Human epididymis protein 4. Ovarian cancer biomarker, more specific than CA-125. ROMA algorithm combines HE4 + CA-125.
text_chunk: HE4 (normal <70 pmol/L premenopausal, <140 postmenopausal). More specific than CA-125 for ovarian cancer. ROMA algorithm (HE4 + CA-125 + menopausal status) classifies pelvic masses as high vs low risk.

name: Free Fatty Acids
clinical_significance: Non-esterified fatty acids. Elevated in insulin resistance, obesity. Lipotoxic to beta-cells. Early metabolic syndrome marker.
text_chunk: Free Fatty Acids (0.1-0.6 mmol/L fasting). Elevated FFA reflects insulin resistance (insulin normally suppresses lipolysis). Chronically elevated FFA cause beta-cell lipotoxicity and hepatic steatosis.

name: Omega-6/Omega-3 Ratio
clinical_significance: Dietary fatty acid balance. Western diet ratio 15-20:1 (pro-inflammatory). Optimal 1-4:1. FADS1/2 variants affect ratio.
text_chunk: Omega-6/Omega-3 Ratio (optimal 1-4:1, Western diet typically 15-20:1). High ratio promotes pro-inflammatory eicosanoid production. FADS1 and FADS2 variants affect desaturase activity. Increasing EPA+DHA intake is the primary strategy.

name: FibroScan (LSM)
clinical_significance: Liver stiffness measurement by transient elastography. <7 kPa normal, 7-10 possible fibrosis, 10-14 probable fibrosis, >14 cirrhosis.
text_chunk: FibroScan LSM (<7 kPa normal, 7-10 possible fibrosis, 10-14 probable fibrosis, >14 cirrhosis). Non-invasive liver fibrosis assessment. PNPLA3 GG carriers should have FibroScan annually. Complements FIB-4 for indeterminate scores (1.3-2.67).

name: MPV
clinical_significance: Mean platelet volume. Larger platelets are more metabolically active and thrombogenic. Elevated MPV associated with increased CV events and mortality.
text_chunk: MPV (7.5-11.5 fL). Larger platelets are more metabolically active. Elevated MPV is associated with increased risk of MI, stroke, and CV mortality. Low MPV with thrombocytopenia suggests bone marrow failure.

name: Nucleated RBC
clinical_significance: Normally absent in peripheral blood. Presence indicates severe anemia, bone marrow stress, extramedullary hematopoiesis, or critical illness.
text_chunk: Nucleated RBCs (normally 0/100 WBC in adults). Presence in peripheral blood is pathological and indicates severe bone marrow stress (hemolysis, hemorrhage), extramedullary hematopoiesis, or critical illness. Associated with increased ICU mortality.

name: Oxidized LDL
clinical_significance: Oxidatively modified LDL. Directly atherogenic - promotes foam cell formation. Marker of oxidative stress. Reduced by antioxidants and statins.
text_chunk: Oxidized LDL (26-117 U/L). Directly promotes atherosclerosis through macrophage scavenger receptor uptake and foam cell formation. PON1 variants affect LDL oxidation susceptibility. Statins and Mediterranean diet reduce oxLDL.

name: Resistin
clinical_significance: Pro-inflammatory adipokine. Elevated in obesity and insulin resistance. In humans, primarily from macrophages. Associated with atherosclerosis.
text_chunk: Resistin (4-12 ng/mL). In humans, primarily secreted by macrophages. Elevated resistin promotes insulin resistance and inflammation. RETN gene promoter variants affect expression. Associated with increased CV risk.

name: Beta-2 Microglobulin
clinical_significance: Component of MHC class I. Elevated in renal failure, lymphoproliferative disorders (myeloma, CLL). Used in myeloma staging (ISS).
text_chunk: Beta-2 Microglobulin (0.8-2.2 mg/L). Elevated in renal impairment and lymphoproliferative disorders. International Staging System for myeloma: Stage I <3.5, Stage III >5.5 mg/L. Also a marker of immune activation.

name: CRP Quantitative
clinical_significance: Standard CRP for infection/acute inflammation monitoring. >10 mg/dL suggests bacterial infection. Different from hs-CRP used for CV risk.
text_chunk: CRP Quantitative (normal <0.5 mg/dL). Detects acute inflammation and infection. >10 mg/dL strongly suggests bacterial infection. Different from hs-CRP (mg/L scale) for CV risk. CRP rises within 6-8 hours of insult, peaks at 48 hours.
